Tium eremitum is a plant species that belongs to the genus Tium, a group of flowering plants in the family Boraginaceae.

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. 

The bioactive constituents of Tium eremitum include iridoid glycosides, such as tiumoside, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Tium eremitum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions.
